meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: My dreams in Iran were already dead before the ceasefire came
items.summary: The bombs have stopped falling for now. But the damage is done, and my life has been wrecked.

items.title: US, Iran exchange threats as fragile ceasefire set to expire
items.summary: The US and Iran trade threats as tensions escalate between the countries before their two-week ceasefire ends.

items.title: Tennis duo Sabalenka and Alcaraz sweep top Laureus sports honours
items.summary: Barcelona's Yamal bags Young Sportsperson of the Year accolade a year after winning the Breakthrough award in 2025.

items.title: Virginia redistricting vote: What polls suggest and what voters will decide
items.summary: The race is tight, and a win could result in more seats in Congress for the Democrats.

items.title: Mughal-era pigeon training survives in heart of India’s capital
items.summary: Pigeon-keeping remains a therapeutic and communal escape for Delhi residents amid the city's chaos and demands.

items.title: Trump’s Board of Peace holds Gaza reconstruction talks with UAE’s DP World
items.summary: Israel has killed more than 700 people in Gaza since last year's US-brokered 'ceasefire'.

items.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 53 of the US-Israel conflict?
items.summary: Diplomatic efforts to end the US-Israel war on Iran are stalled as Tehran rejects talks under pressure.

items.title: Technofacism? Why Palantir’s pro-West ‘manifesto’ has critics alarmed
items.summary: Palantir's 'manifesto' has been described as an 'AI-driven threat to humanity’s existence' and 'technofascism'.

items.title: Israeli police arrest two ultra-Orthodox Jews for removing Israeli flags
items.summary: Two ultra-Orthodox Jews tore down Israeli flags in Beit Shemesh, before being arrested by Israeli authorities.

items.title: Iran war forces job losses, reverse migration in India’s ceramic hub
items.summary: The ceramics industry in Gujarat's Morbi has been badly hit by fuel crisis, with most units closed and workers laid off.

items.title: US veterans arrested in Capitol during protest against the war on Iran
items.summary: US veterans were arrested while staging a protest in the US Capitol building in opposition to the war on Iran.

items.title: Amnesty International slams ‘predatory world order’ led by US and Israel
items.summary: Amnesty’s Secretary General says the US, Israel and Russia have waged war on civilians with impunity.

items.title: Japan lifts ban on lethal weapons exports in major shift of pacifist policy
items.summary: Japan could soon sell weapons overseas, including fighter jets, in a shift from its decades-old pacifist policy.

items.title: Algeria’s ex-minister of industry jailed in high-profile corruption case
items.summary: Former minister among several people jailed in corruption case that saw prison sentences of between three and 10 years.

items.title: CJ McCollum, Hawks rally late to shock Knicks in Game 2 of NBA playoffs
items.summary: Led by guard McCollum, the Atlanta Hawks staged a late comeback to upset New York Knicks to tie their series 1-1.

items.title: Trump’s Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer latest to leave administration
items.summary: Chavez-DeRemer leaves the Trump administration after the recent departures of Kristi Noem and Pam Bondi.

items.title: Cuba confirms talks with US officials, wants end to Trump’s energy blockade
items.summary: A Cuban Foreign Ministry official said exchange with Washington was 'respectful and professional' and devoid of threats.

items.title: Global Sumud Flotilla disrupts cargo ship bound for Israel
items.summary: Activists with the Global Sumud Flotilla have disrupted a cargo ship they say carried materials for Israeli weapons.

items.title: US is ‘pretty far behind where they started’ the war on Iran
items.summary: Daniel Benaim says the US missed the early off-ramps to declare victory over Iran.

items.title: Global fallout grows over Israeli soldier smashing Jesus statue in Lebanon
items.summary: Outrage continues to grow over the Israeli soldier who was photographed desecrating a statue of Jesus Christ in Lebanon.

items.title: Six women win 2026 Goldman prize, world’s top environmental award
items.summary: First all-women cohort of winners hails from Colombia, Nigeria, Papua New Guinea, South Korea, the UK and the US.

items.title: Iran war live: Tehran spurns talks under threats; Trump says blockade stays
items.summary: Iran says will not accept negotiations 'under the shadow of threats', as Trump promises to continue blockade in Hormuz.

items.title: ‘Predators’: Amnesty slams Netanyahu, Putin, Trump as human rights decline
items.summary: Global rights group says leaders of three countries behind devastating wars inspire other states to carry out abuses.

items.title: Hungary’s incoming PM says Netanyahu would be arrested if he visits
items.summary: Hungary’s election winner says he would enact an ICC arrest warrant for Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu and detain him.

items.title: Singer D4vd charged with murder of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez
items.summary: Singer faces first-degree murder and additional charges that could lead to life without parole or the death penalty.
